side_effects.description: Possible contribution to rapid cancer cell division and aggressive growth rate; angiogenesis may have accelerated existing cancer progression

protocol_notes: Two courses of BPC-157 over two years. Oral administration. User speculates peptide may have contributed to accelerated cancer growth through angiogenesis, though acknowledges cancer likely has hormonal basis and no definitive causal link can be established. User clarifies in edits that BPC-157 likely did not cause cancer but may have promoted rapid cell division once cancer was present.
